meaning: adj.(关于)(美国第三十七任总统)尼克松(或其政策)的,像尼克松似的
examples: Avoiding a US-China conflict will take Nixonian dexterity.
They are risks that threaten to reduce Mr Bush to Nixonian levels of isolation, and that threaten long-term damage to the Republican Party.
Mr McCain's running-mate, Sarah Palin, is a Nixonian fantasy come true, perfectly designed to create a cycle of accusation and counteraccusation.
It was part of a tried-and-true Nixonian strategy: Run to the extreme right in the primaries, then run as fast as you can to the middle in the general election.
